stories.body: President Donald Trump signed three proclamations imposing 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods in 30 days, accusing Ottawa of discriminating against U.S. automobiles, alcohol and dairy products, while excluding energy, potash, fish and critical minerals; Prime Minister Mark Carney said Canada is ready to intensify talks even as prior U.S. tariffs on steel, aluminum and lumber remain in place and Canada keeps 25% countermeasures on select U.S. products.
stories.headline: Trump orders sweeping 50% tariffs on Canadian imports

stories.body: U.S. Central Command said fresh strikes in Iran targeted command centers, maritime assets and missile and drone launch sites around cities such as Tabriz and Chabahar after President Trump vowed retaliation for three slain U.S. soldiers, while Iran claimed it hit two oil tankers plus U.S. military sites in Bahrain and Kuwait and the State Department issued a worldwide caution that Iranian actors could target Americans or diplomatic facilities anywhere.
stories.headline: U.S. hits Iran for 10th straight night as global caution issued

stories.body: Iranian state media reported explosions in Bandar Abbas, Qeshm Island, Chabahar and Konarak as Tehran said it had attacked Bahrain and Kuwait and left two tankers ablaze in the Strait of Hormuz, framing the moves as part of an escalating confrontation with the United States.
stories.headline: Iran claims strikes on Bahrain and Kuwait amid Hormuz tanker attacks

stories.body: French lawmakers are poised to pass legislation that would bar children younger than 15 from using social media platforms, potentially making France the first European country with such a prohibition after the upper and lower houses agreed on compromise language ahead of Tuesday’s vote.
stories.headline: France set to ban social media access for children under 15

stories.body: Uganda began the World Health Organization’s 42-day countdown to declare its Ebola outbreak over after releasing its final patient, prompting Health Minister Chris Baryomunsi to lobby the 15 countries that still restrict travel despite only 20 infections and two deaths, while officials highlighted investments in preparedness and ongoing trials for Bundibugyo-strain vaccines as the neighboring DRC continues to report rising cases.
stories.headline: Uganda urges end to Ebola travel bans after last patient discharged

stories.body: During the 47th anniversary of the Sandinista revolution, President Daniel Ortega declared there will be no more elections in Nicaragua to prevent the opposition from taking power, saying he would work with the National Assembly to erect legal barriers against “traitors,” a move analysts say cements the Murillo-Ortega family dictatorship after years of suppressing rivals and constitutional changes that extended presidential terms and elevated Rosario Murillo to co-president.
stories.headline: Nicaragua’s Ortega vows to end elections entirely

stories.body: Guyana’s prime minister said 27 people died, 69 were rescued and 83 remain missing after the MV Barima ferry capsized Saturday night en route from Georgetown to the Essequibo region, prompting an ongoing search-and-rescue effort.
stories.headline: At least 27 dead, 83 missing in Guyana ferry disaster

stories.body: Authorities say 43-year-old Andrew Arrabaca, a former Patriot missile mechanic with anti-ICE views, ignited fireworks and a fire and allegedly fired a pellet gun outside Manhattan’s Javits Federal Building at 26 Federal Plaza, triggering an FBI-led terrorism probe amid heightened tensions over mass deportation operations; responders extinguished the blaze and arrested a suspect as officials stressed the building houses multiple agencies including immigration courts.
stories.headline: Army veteran accused of anti-ICE attack at Manhattan federal building

stories.body: Republican Rep. Nancy Mace said in a video message she will not run in the special election to succeed the late Sen. Lindsey Graham, citing her current battles on behalf of women’s rights after a poor showing in this year’s gubernatorial primary, leaving Trump-backed interim Sen. Darline Graham and Reps. Russell Fry and Ralph Norman as leading GOP contenders ahead of the Aug. 11 primary.
stories.headline: Nancy Mace opts out of race for Lindsey Graham’s Senate seat

stories.body: Influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate were detained by U.S. Marshals in Miami on a sealed U.K. warrant tied to accusations they raped and trafficked women between 2010 and 2017, appeared in federal court, and now await a judge’s decision on extradition as their lawyer Joseph McBride denounced the British case as politically motivated and the brothers remain in federal detention.
stories.headline: Tate brothers arrested in Miami, challenge UK extradition on trafficking charges

stories.body: In newly submitted cooperation briefs, Plus Ultra president Julio Martínez Sola, CEO Roberto Roselli and businessman Julio Martínez Martínez told Audiencia Nacional investigators that former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero guided their lobbying for a €53 million state rescue approved in March 2021 and expected a 1% success fee routed through Martínez’s firm Análisis Relevante, contradicting Zapatero’s prior denials and suggesting the influence scheme was replicated with other companies.
stories.headline: Plus Ultra executives implicate Zapatero in 2021 bailout commissions

stories.body: The forest fire that began Thursday in La Mierla has scorched more than 26,000 hectares across a 120-kilometer perimeter, forcing evacuations in at least 33 Guadalajara municipalities and prompting Castilla y León to reinforce defenses in Soria amid toxic smoke that already led to Barcones’ evacuation, while 379 personnel, 57 ground units and 29 aircraft focus on protecting towns and attacking the fire’s head near Atienza.
stories.headline: La Mierla wildfire becomes Castilla-La Mancha’s largest on record

stories.body: Following the EU Court of Justice’s green light for Spain’s amnesty law, the Tribunal de Cuentas ended its suspension but asked prosecutors, civic groups and defense teams to detail within 10 days whether funds tied to the 2017 referendum and Catalan foreign outreach came from EU budgets, noting that European money would block amnesty for the more than €3 million in alleged accounting liability involving Carles Puigdemont, Artur Mas, Oriol Junqueras and other former officials.
stories.headline: Tribunal de Cuentas seeks new filings before applying Catalan amnesty

stories.body: A protected witness who helped dismantle a drug-trafficking network died without receiving more than €500,000 owed for his cooperation because Spain’s Interior and Justice ministries spent 16 years disputing which department was competent to pay despite court rulings recognizing the debt, underscoring a bureaucratic stalemate that outlived the informant.
stories.headline: Protected witness dies after 16-year dispute over €500,000 reward

stories.body: Barcelona’s city hall and the Catalan government said recorded offenses fell 11.2% year over year thanks to fewer thefts and knife incidents, though they cautioned that pressure from organized gangs still requires sustained policing efforts.
stories.headline: Reported crime in Barcelona drops 11.2% in first half of 2026

stories.body: Mossos d’Esquadra opened a homicide investigation after a man who allegedly breached a restraining order against his partner was stabbed in Barcelona’s Barceloneta neighborhood during the night, later dying in hospital, with no arrests yet announced.
stories.headline: Man fatally stabbed in Ciutat Vella after violating restraining order

stories.body: Barcelona transit operator TMB, working with disability federation Dincat, released a validated easy-read metro guide that explains signage, platform navigation and transfers to help people with intellectual disabilities—along with children, seniors and newcomers—plan trips independently as part of a broader universal accessibility plan.
stories.headline: TMB issues easy-read metro guide for riders with intellectual disabilities

stories.body: Barcelona Activa’s Cibernàrium launched over 100 free short courses totaling more than 270 training hours—many on-demand and under six hours—covering artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, data analysis, programming and digital productivity, supplemented by live virtual sessions and August in-person classes that together provide 400 seats and aim to surpass last year’s 12,700 participants.
stories.headline: Cibernàrium offers 100+ free summer tech courses
